response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack and systems engineer with broad experience in event-driven architectures, high-performance services, and agile practices, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirement. C++ is conspicuously absent from his listed skills, and his only verifiable C++ work is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor thesis project. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise as its primary technical pillar, alongside industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443-4-1), industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and a secure development lifecycle mindset — none of which appear anywhere in this resume.
strengths: Strong background in high-performance, event-driven, and distributed systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s; GISCollective: event-driven geo-data platform)
Demonstrated culture of quality engineering: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a track record of mentoring through workshops and code dojos
Hands-on experience with networked, async, concurrent systems via DLang (async I/O, fibers) which maps partially to multi-threading requirements
concerns: C++ is absent from the resume's skills section and has not been used professionally in over 10 years — the role's single most critical technical requirement is effectively unmet
Zero evidence of secure development lifecycle experience, IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, or any industrial-protocol knowledge (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No industrial automation domain experience beyond a brief junior-level role at National Instruments; the candidate's entire career trajectory points toward web, SaaS, and cloud-native development
